---
title: "geracao_energia_eletrica"
author: "Fernando Almeida Barbalho"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(colorspace)
library(ggrepel)

yearly_full_release_long_format <- 
  read_csv("yearly_full_release_long_format.csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

theme_vpr<- function(paleta_cores){
  
  theme_light() +
    theme(
      panel.background = element_rect(fill= paleta_cores$cor_fundo_painel),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.background = element_rect(fill= paleta_cores$cor_fundo, color = paleta_cores$cor_fundo),
      #legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill =paleta_cores$cor_fundo ),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8, face = "italic"),
      text = element_text( colour = paleta_cores$cor_texto),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = paleta_cores$cor_fundo  ),
      strip.text = element_text(size= 7, face ="bold", color = paleta_cores$cor_texto)
    )  
  
  
}


paleta_cores<- list(  cor_fundo= "#D0D0D0", # "#E0E0E0", # # "#F8F8F8",
                      
                      cor_fundo_painel ="#F5F5F5",# "#D0D0D0",# "#2B2B2B",#  "#F5F5F5",
                      
                      cor_fundo_estados = "#696969",
                      
                      cor_fundo_mapa_pontos = "#2B2B2B", #"#505050"
                      
                      cor_texto =  "#324e5a" #"#F5F5F5"# 
)

```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r fig.dpi= 300}






dados_emissao_geracao<-
  yearly_full_release_long_format %>%
  filter(category %in% c("Electricity generation", "Power sector emissions")  ,
         subcategory == "Aggregate fuel",
         variable %in% c("Clean", "Fossil"),
         between(year, 2019, 2024) ,
         !is.na(iso_3_code),
         unit %in% c("TWh", "mtCO2")) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE),
            .by = c(year, iso_3_code, unit)) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = c(unit) ,
              values_from = total) %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  mutate(emission_per_mwh = mt_co2/t_wh) 


dados_geracao<-
  yearly_full_release_long_format %>%  
  filter(category %in% c("Electricity generation")  ,
         subcategory == "Aggregate fuel",
         variable %in% c("Clean", "Fossil"),
         between(year, 2019, 2024) ,
         !is.na(iso_3_code),
         unit %in% c("TWh")) %>%
  select(year, iso_3_code,variable, value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = variable,
              values_from = value)

top_12_geradores_2024<-
  (dados_emissao_geracao %>%
  filter(year == 2024) %>%
  slice_max(n=6, order_by = t_wh))$iso_3_code


top_12_emissores_2024<-
  (dados_emissao_geracao %>%
  filter(year == 2024,
         iso_3_code %in% top_12_geradores_2024) %>%
  slice_max(n=6, order_by = emission_per_mwh))$iso_3_code


  
dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_previo <-
  dados_emissao_geracao %>%
  inner_join(dados_geracao) %>%
  filter(iso_3_code %in% top_12_geradores_2024) %>%
  mutate(year =as.factor(year),
         iso_3_code = factor(iso_3_code, levels =top_12_emissores_2024 )) 




dados_sel_ano<-
  dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_previo %>%
  filter(year %in% c(2024)) %>%
  mutate(pais_2024 = paste0(iso_3_code, " emissões em 2024: ", scales::number(emission_per_mwh, accuracy = 0.1, decimal.mark = ",", suffix = " mtCO2/TWh"))) %>%
  select(iso_3_code, pais_2024)

dados_graficos_dispersao_ee<-
  dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_previo %>%
  inner_join(dados_sel_ano) 

dados_graficos_dispersao_ee %>%
  ggplot(aes(Clean, Fossil)) +
  geom_path( aes(group = iso_3_code, color = iso_3_code) ,
                  arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.2,"cm"), type = "closed"),
             show.legend = FALSE,
             size =1
             ) + #color = "#4FC3F7"
  geom_point( fill= paleta_cores$cor_texto, pch= 21, color= paleta_cores$cor_fundo_painel, size =2, show.legend = FALSE) +

#   geom_label_repel(aes(label =paste0( ifelse(year %in% c(2019,2024),as.character(year) ,"") ,ifelse(year %in% c(2019,2024),":",""), scales::number(t_wh, accuracy = 1.0, big.mark = ".")),
#                       fill = t_wh,
#                       color = t_wh > 7000),
#                   size =2,
#                   alpha = 1,
#                   fontface = "bold",
#                   show.legend = FALSE,
# ,                 label.padding = 0.1,
#                   ) +

  geom_label_repel(aes(label =paste0( ifelse(year %in% c(2019),as.character(year) ,"") ,ifelse(year %in% c(2019),": ",""), scales::number(t_wh, accuracy = 1.0, big.mark = "."),ifelse(year %in% c(2019)," TwH",""))),
                  size =2.5,
                  alpha = 0.-8+75,
                  #fontface = "bold",
                  color = paleta_cores$cor_texto,
                  show.legend = FALSE,
,                 label.padding = 0.1,
                  ) +
  scale_color_discrete_qualitative(palette = "Dark 2") +
  #scale_fill_continuous_sequential(palette = "Heat 2", labels =   scales::number_format(big.mark = ".")) +
  #scale_color_continuous_sequential(palette = "Heat 2", labels =   scales::number_format(big.mark = ".")) +
  #scale_color_manual(values = c("black","white")) +
  #scale_x_log10() +
  #scale_y_log10() +
  facet_wrap(pais_2024~., scales= "free") +
  theme_vpr(paleta_cores = paleta_cores) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.01,"line"),
    #legend.key.size = unit(0.8,"line")
    legend.key.height = unit(0.4,"line")
  ) +
  labs(
    fill = "Total generated energy in TwH",
    size =  "Emissions in mtCO2/TWh",
    x= "Clean sources in TwH",
    y= "Fossil sources in TwH",
    title = "Clean Power Rising, But Not Everywhere (2019-2024)", 
    subtitle = str_wrap("Clean energy drives growth across major producers. China expands 21% in fossil and 62% in clean. France and Brazil remain low-emission benchmarks, while Canada, Germany, and Iran show setbacks.", 100),
    caption = "Years from 2019 to 2024. Source: Ember. Elaboration by Fernando Barbalho"
    
  )

```
